threatgraph_indicators.description: A process attempted to write a malicious payload. Review the process tree.

A process has written a suspicious file to disk. Adversaries may write a malicious file to a commonly trusted directory, use a benign name, or a mismatched file extension. This is done for the sake of evading defenses and observation. Check the activity and surrounding events are expected in your environment.
An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.

description: A process accessed system network configuration settings. Adversaries can use this for lateral movement efforts. Review the process tree.
name: GenericSystemNetworkConfigDiscovery

description: User hashes from SAM hive accessed from signed executable with untrusted root authority
name: SAMHashDump.Untrusted

description: A process gathered information about the operating system or hardware. Adversaries can use this to identify system vulnerabilities. Review the process tree.
name: GenericSystemInfoDiscovery

threatgraph_indicators.description: User hashes from SAM hive accessed from signed executable with untrusted root authority

A process gathered information about the operating system or hardware. Adversaries can use this to identify system vulnerabilities. Review the process tree.
An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.
A process associated with a known ransomware campaign launched. Investigate the host for signs of a ransomware attack.

threatgraph_indicators.description: A process has opened a handle to a Falcon process with excessive permissions requested. If this is unexpected, review the process tree and surrounding events.
A process appears to be modifying Component Object Model (COM) objects in the registry. This can be used by attackers to persist malicious code. Investigate the registry operations and process tree.
An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.

name: SensorHandleOpenedLow
